mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate raises equity present values and eases financing conditions, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the rate is moving down rather than merely level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upside CPIAUCSL surprise indicates inflation persistence and should lift real-rate and discount-rate expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week when inflation is accelerating above its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE after a low point signals weakening labor demand and slower earnings growth, which should pressure equities downward over the next month once unemployment has clearly turned up.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger PAYEMS growth together with rising RSAFS indicates broad demand and revenue expansion, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings breadth improves beyond simple labor strength alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone on 60-day forward returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and cyclical activity, so improving production should lead positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when manufacturing momentum is expanding.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or if the signal's information coefficient is indistinguishable from zero over any 24-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand contraction and weaker construction activity, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when housing momentum turns down materially.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-decline triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional median return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth weakens while PAYEMS growth also stalls, the economy is moving into broad demand slowdown that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because both output and income creation are fading.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-payroll weakness does not outperform each series alone in predicting negative 60-day returns, or if the interaction effect changes sign in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, FEDFUNDS above neutral and CPIAUCSL still elevated implies restrictive real financing conditions that compress equity multiples, so the next month should have negative S&P 500 returns until inflation and policy rates both cool.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and elevated CPIAUCSL do not show negative 20-day forward returns more often than not, or if the joint signal does not beat a FEDFUNDS-only benchmark.
